title: Crepidiastrum sonchifolium
taxonomy: Crepidiastrum sonchifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the tribe Cichorieae and is thought to be closely related to the genus Crepis. The genus Crepidiastrum is often considered a synonym of Crepis, but some sources recognize it as a distinct genus. Further research is needed to clarify the classification of Crepidiastrum sonchifolium. The plant's classification can be complex due to ongoing debate among taxonomists.

intro: Crepidiastrum sonchifolium, also known as Japanese dandelion or Japanese sowthistle, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Crepidiastrum sonchifolium include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
